turns.content: Help me plan my week. I have a dentist appointment Tuesday, need to finish the API redesign by Thursday, and want to prep for the team retrospective on Friday.

📋 SUMMARY: Weekly planning for Feb 11-15 with three key priorities.

Here's your week at a glance, Jm:

**Monday (Today - Feb 11)**
- Wind down current work, review API redesign scope
- Identify blockers for the redesign so Tuesday/Wednesday are productive

**Tuesday (Feb 12)**
- Dentist appointment
- Light coding around the appointment — tackle smaller API redesign tasks (schema updates, type definitions, tests)

**Wednesday (Feb 13)**
- Deep focus day — core API redesign implementation
- Goal: have the bulk of changes working locally by end of day

**Thursday (Feb 14)**
- Morning: finish API redesign, run full test suite, PR review
- Afternoon: buffer time for fixes, merge
- Deadline: API redesign done

**Friday (Feb 15)**
- Morning: prep retro notes — what went well, what didn't, action items
- Team retrospective
- Week wrap-up

**Key risks:**
1. API redesign is the heaviest item — Wednesday is your make-or-break day
2. If the dentist runs long Tuesday, you lose prep time — front-load what you can today
3. Retro prep is lightweight but easy to forget — block 30 min Friday morning

Want me to add any of these to your calendar or create tasks in LucidTasks?

🗣️ Kaya: Three priorities mapped across the week. Wednesday is your critical focus day.
